citation.sourceDocument: Minnesota DOR 2026 income tax rate schedules (indexed) and standard deduction
citation.sourceNote: Minnesota's four rates (5.35% / 6.8% / 7.85% / 9.85%) are the same for every filing status, but the thresholds differ: single crosses to 6.8% at $33,310, married-filing-jointly at $48,700, head-of-household at $41,010 (7.85% at $109,430 / $193,480 / $164,800; 9.85% at $203,150 / $337,930 / $270,060). These are the official 2026 figures from the Minnesota Department of Revenue's December 2025 announcement; the brackets and standard deduction are indexed for inflation each year. The 2026 standard deduction is $15,300 single / $30,600 married / $23,000 head of household. Minnesota has no personal exemption (TCJA conformity); its $5,300-per-dependent exemption is omitted for the no-dependent filer, and the standard-deduction phase-out for high earners (above roughly $239,000 of income) is omitted too — both make the figure err slightly high, the conservative side. Minnesota levies no local income tax. The brackets and standard deduction index together annually; the refresh adapter anchors the standard deduction and the per-status bracket tables roll alongside it as the reviewer's data-only step. Verified against the MN DOR on 2026-06-06.
